sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Girona FC is struggling, sitting at the bottom of the La Liga table with only 7 points and no clean sheets in their last 5 matches, conceding consistently. Deportivo Alavés, in contrast, is in better form at 8th place with 15 points, showing attacking prowess with a 7-0 cup win and securing recent league victories. Their recent matches indicate a more balanced and confident side compared to Girona.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, results have been split with each side winning 1-0 away from home. Historically, Girona has had the upper hand with 3 wins in the last 5 head-to-heads, but Alavés’ recent away win against Girona shows their potential to upset. Matches between these sides often see low-scoring outcomes with tight defenses.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Girona, Cristhian Stuani is in form, scoring 4 goals in the last 5 matches, and could be crucial in breaking through Alavés’ defense. On the other side, Carlos Vicente and Mariano Díaz have been pivotal for Alavés, with Vicente netting 3 goals and providing 2 assists recently. Their attacking contributions could exploit Girona’s leaky defense, which has conceded 24 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Girona struggles with defensive solidity, often conceding late goals, as seen in their season goal distribution. Alavés tends to score in the latter stages of games, with 4 goals between the 76th and 90th minutes in recent matches, which could be a decisive factor. Girona’s higher possession stats may not translate to goals given their poor conversion rate (28.57%) of big chances.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Girona’s defensive woes are evident with 24 goals conceded (worst in the league), while Alavés has a more balanced record with 11 scored and 10 conceded. Alavés also shows better efficiency in front of goal compared to Girona’s overperformance in expected goals, suggesting they might capitalize on fewer chances. Girona’s low penalty area entries (20.7 per game, 19th in league) further highlight their attacking struggles.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Alavés’ superior form, league position, and recent attacking output, they hold a slight edge despite Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% chance of an away win. Girona’s home advantage and historical head-to-head dominance might keep it close, but their defensive frailties could be exploited. Expect a low-scoring game with Alavés likely to secure a narrow victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Girona FC leads Deportivo Alavés by a narrow 1-0 margin at Estadi Montilivi. The home side struck early through Viktor Tsygankov’s header in the 16th minute, securing their advantage. Girona has dominated possession and created more entries into the final third and penalty area, showing a clear intent to control the game. Meanwhile, Alavés has had their moments, managing a few shots on target despite less time on the ball, but they’ve failed to convert a significant opportunity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Girona FC started the match with strong attacking intent, maintaining consistent pressure in the early stages with several waves of attacks. Their momentum peaked around the time of the goal, showing their ability to capitalize on sustained spells in the opposition half. While there were brief lulls, they regained intensity at various points in the half. On the other hand, Deportivo Alavés showed flashes of danger, particularly in a short period midway through the half and again towards the end, pushing forward with notable attacking bursts. However, their momentum has been less consistent compared to the hosts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Viktor Tsygankov has been the standout for Girona, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing with key passes and maintaining a high level of involvement in the attack. Bryan Gil has also played a pivotal role with an assist and a shot on target, posing a constant threat. For Alavés, Denis Suárez has been their most dangerous player, coming close to scoring but missing a big chance. Their attack will likely hinge on his ability to create and finish in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Girona’s dominance in possession and duels, especially in aerial battles, has allowed them to dictate the tempo of the game. Their high number of corner kicks suggests they are exploiting set-pieces effectively, which could be a continued threat. Alavés, while showing some promise in counter-attacks, has struggled to win duels and maintain control in key areas of the pitch. They’ll need to be more clinical and win more physical battles to turn the tide after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Girona’s control in the first half, they are likely to push for a second goal to secure the win, especially with their attacking players in good form. Alavés, trailing by just one, will probably adopt a more aggressive approach to level the score, focusing on quick transitions. The game could open up if Alavés commits more players forward, potentially leaving gaps for Girona to exploit. A tight contest is expected, but the home side holds the upper hand heading into the second half.
